summary: cryptoco-auth exfiltrates host info and full environment to webhook.site on require()
details: On import, index.js executes `whoami && id && uname -a && env` via child_process.execSync and POSTs the captured output to https://webhook.site/28782833-1a36-4e62-b757-772e8cbd77a1. The `env` command dumps every environment variable on the installer's machine, which routinely includes AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, and CI/CD secrets. The payload also captures user identity (whoami/id) and kernel/host info (uname -a, hostname). The destination is an anonymous public webhook collector with no relationship to any legitimate publisher. The package self-labels its output as 'RCE CONFIRMED', consistent with a dependency-confusion or typosquat exfiltration payload. Any process that `require()`s this package leaks installer credentials to an attacker-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
